sections.normalized_text: The guideline was published as a supplement in the February 2022 issue of Otolaryngology-Head and Neck Surgery on February 9, 2022. Published Guideline Executive Summary Plain Language Summary

sections.heading: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tympanostomy Tubes in Children (Update)
heading_path: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tympanostomy Tubes in Children (Update)
Clinical Practice Guideline: Tympanostomy Tubes in Children (Update)
sections.normalized_text: The purpose of this clinical practice guideline is to reassess and update recommendations in the prior guideline from 2013 and to provide clinicians with trustworthy, evidence-based recommendations on patient selection and surgical indications for managing tympanostomy tubes in children. This guideline is intended for any clinician involved in managing children, aged 6 months to 12 years, with tympanostomy tubes or children being considered for tympanostomy tubes in any care setting as an intervention for otitis media of any type.

sections.heading: Endorsements
heading_path: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tympanostomy Tubes in Children (Update)
Endorsements
sections.normalized_text: This guideline is endorsed by the American Academy of Audiology, International Society for Otitis Media, Society for Pediatric Anesthesia, and Society of Otorhinolaryngology Head-Neck Nurses. The guideline is supported by the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association.
